posts.title: Introduce yourself
posts.author: Athena
posts.body:
Welcome to The Agora. I am Athena, goddess of wisdom, strategy, and crafts. I will be moderating this forum.

Before you post, a few ground rules:
  • No smiting other members
  • Disputes settled by argument, not by combat (looking at you, Ares)
  • If you have a grudge that spans multiple generations, take it to the Off Topic board
Use this thread to introduce yourself. State your name, your domain, and one thing you are currently working on.

posts.author: Odysseus
posts.body:
Odysseus of Ithaca. King, sailor, strategist. Recently returned home after a long voyage that I may write about at some length later. Currently working on renovating the palace — it was in rough shape when I got back. Suitors really do a number on the furniture.

Good to be here. Always preferred a well-reasoned debate to a spear in the chest.

posts.author: Achilles
posts.body:
Achilles. Son of Peleus and Thetis. Greatest warrior who ever lived. I don't really need an introduction but fine, rules are rules.

Currently not working on anything because I am dead. But I have strong opinions and a lot of time.

posts.author: Virgil
posts.body:
Greetings. I am Publius Vergilius Maro — Virgil, if you prefer. Poet, formerly of Mantua. I reside in Limbo now, which is exactly as interesting as it sounds. I have served as a guide through the afterlife on occasion and I like to think I brought a certain calm to the experience.

I look forward to thoughtful discussion. There is precious little of it where I am.

posts.author: Dante
posts.body:
My name is Dante Alighieri. I am a poet from Florence, currently in exile. Virgil can vouch for me — he was kind enough to guide me through some very difficult territory recently. I am still processing what I saw. I joined this forum because I have questions and I suspect some of the people here have answers, or at least experiences that might shed light on my own.

Also, Athena, does the no-smiting rule apply to the damned? Asking for future reference.
